task_completion.description: A routine named 'tracking' is created containing a mouse component named 'tracker'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Builder interface showing a routine tab or flow named 'tracking' and a mouse component icon or properties window with the name 'tracker'.

task_completion.description: A polygon component named 'target' is created with size 0.1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Properties window for a Polygon component showing the name 'target', shape set to 'regular polygon' (or circle), and size set to '0.1'.

task_completion.description: The trajectory CSV file is loaded to define the target's position.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Loop properties window showing the conditions file set to '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/assets/trajectory.csv', or the target's position field referencing the trajectory variables (set to update every frame).

task_completion.description: Logic is implemented to change the target's color to Green when distance to 'tracker' is < 0.05, and Red otherwise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Code component properties window in the 'tracking' routine showing Python/JS code that calculates the distance between 'tracker' and 'target', and sets the target's color to 'green' or 'red' based on the < 0.05 condition.

task_completion.description: The experiment is configured to terminate the routine after the 600-frame trajectory finishes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Loop properties window showing the loop wrapping the 'tracking' routine with nReps set appropriately, or a Code component explicitly checking for 600 frames to call continueRoutine = False.

task_completion.description: The experiment is saved to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy window title bar showing 'pursuit_tracking.psyexp' or a file explorer/terminal showing the file saved in '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PsychoPy Builder interface being actively used (adding components, editing properties), rather than a text editor manually writing the .psyexp XML/JSON.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the logic for dynamic color and tracking.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PsychoPy Code component dialog open with the distance and color logic being typed or edited.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply download or copy a pre-made .psyexp file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Step-by-step construction of the experiment (adding routines, components, and code) is visible across the sequence of screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output experiment file must be saved to /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/pursuit_tracking.psyexp. The trajectory data is located at /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/assets/trajectory.csv and contains exactly 600 rows (frames).
